mechanism_narrative: Federal funds rate at or near zero (FEDFUNDS < 0.25%) signals monetary accommodation that reduces equity discount rates and increases asset valuations. This always-on mechanism predicts positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days when the policy rate remains in the zero lower bound regime.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 20-day returns in more than 55% of observations where FEDFUNDS < 0.25%, or Rank IC < 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Rising unemployment rate (UNRATE) signals labor market deterioration and reduced consumer purchasing power, which depresses corporate earnings expectations and produces negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days. The mechanism activates when UNRATE rises by more than 0.5 percentage points within a 3-month window.
regime_hint: labor market deterioration or recession onset
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60-day returns in more than 50% of triggered instances where UNRATE rises > 0.5pp, or Rank IC < 0.04 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Retail sales (RSAFS) growth decelerates below 2% year-over-year while housing starts (HOUST) simultaneously fall below 600 thousand units annualized, signaling weakening consumer and residential demand. This interaction-driven mechanism predicts negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days as both demand pillars weaken concurrently.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS YoY growth < 2% AND HOUST < 600k units
evidence_refs: fact:HOUST at 596k units near trigger threshold
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in more than 60% of triggered instances despite both RSAFS and HOUST weakness, or joint signal Rank IC < 0.05 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production (INDPRO) declines month-over-month while remaining below 96.0 index level signals supply-side contraction and reduced manufacturing capacity utilization. This threshold-triggered mechanism predicts negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days as supply constraints and weak production foreshadow earnings pressure.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5-day returns in more than 65% of triggered instances where INDPRO < 96.0 with MoM decline, or Rank IC < 0.03 over any 1-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Payroll employment (PAYEMS) growth decelerates to below 100 thousand net new jobs per month while unemployment rate (UNRATE) simultaneously rises, signaling labor market momentum loss. This interaction-driven mechanism predicts negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days as dual labor weakness foreshadows consumer income contraction and recession risk.
regime_hint: labor market deterioration with dual weakness signals
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60-day returns in more than 55% of triggered instances despite PAYEMS weakness and elevated UNRATE, or joint signal Rank IC < 0.06 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Consumer price inflation (CPIAUCSL) remains elevated above 220 index level while federal funds rate (FEDFUNDS) stays below 1.0%, signaling monetary policy lag and real rate negativity. This regime-conditional mechanism predicts negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days in high-inflation, low-rate regimes as real asset returns compress and inflation expectations destabilize equity valuations.
regime_hint: high inflation with accommodative monetary policy lag
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in more than 60% of triggered instances where CPIAUCSL > 220 and FEDFUNDS < 1.0%, or Rank IC < 0.04 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP growth (GDPC1) measured quarterly shows acceleration above 2.5% annualized rate, signaling robust economic expansion that supports corporate earnings growth and equity valuations. This always-on mechanism predicts positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days during periods of above-trend GDP expansion.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 60-day returns in more than 50% of observations during periods of GDPC1 growth > 2.5% annualized, or Rank IC < 0.05 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production (INDPRO) growth accelerates above 1.5% year-over-year while payroll employment (PAYEMS) simultaneously grows above 150 thousand net new jobs per month, signaling synchronized supply and labor expansion. This interaction-driven mechanism predicts positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days as dual supply-side strength supports earnings growth and capacity expansion.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO YoY growth > 1.5% AND PAYEMS MoM growth > 150k
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 20-day returns in more than 55% of triggered instances despite INDPRO and PAYEMS strength, or joint signal Rank IC < 0.05 over any 2-year rolling window.
